A.reason: The trigger is simple, but running a spec sweep on every branch advance is costly and may inherit model dependence.

C.reason: Passage hashes make canon-change invalidation rare, cheap, deterministic, and model-free, with only modest false-positive risk from harmless edits.

E.reason: The gate reuses existing chain, pins, and tests, but combining four validations and adapting the sweep suite adds moderate complexity.

G.reason: A pulse-time record diff is cheap, deterministic, and gap-resistant, though verified-closure plumbing must remain reliable.

B.reason: Repository counting is cheap, but discovering every duplicated definition is an open-ended inventory problem needing new machinery and judgment.

D.reason: Hash recomputation is mechanical, but the signed ledger, event integration, and daily sweep add machinery and legitimate-rename noise.

F.reason: Emitter-side SHA and pin checks reuse existing infrastructure, cost little at pulse cadence, and require no semantic judgment.

note: The most durable idea is content-hashed waiver invalidation because a rare deterministic canon-change check can run for years without a model or continuous scanning.
